page: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-1 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring a single dedicated vCPU mapped to one physical core of an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It features 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Supported instruction sets include AVX, AES, VAES, and SHA_NI, while AVX-512 and AMX extensions are absent.

Performance benchmarks reflect the single-core nature of the hardware. The instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 25%, and average single-threaded performance in gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, because it is limited to a single vCPU, multi-core benchmarks such as stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core fall into the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively. Memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with both small-block and large-block read operations scoring in the bottom 10%. Similarly, database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput are constrained, registering in the lowest performance tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the t2d-standard-1 provides an economical entry point for workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources rather than shared-core burstable performance. The dedicated AMD EPYC core ensures predictable execution times for single-threaded tasks, avoiding the noisy-neighbor issues common in shared-core environments. This instance is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic text processing, simple web serving, and low-throughput background tasks that do not demand high memory bandwidth or multi-core parallelization.
description: The Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-1 is a general-purpose instance configured with 1 dedicated vCPU and 4.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, 32 MB of L3 cache, and no bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data reveals strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests, alongside average single-threaded compression speeds. However, its single-core design limits multi-core performance, and memory bandwidth remains in the bottom 10%. This instance offers a cost-effective, predictable option for workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation without the overhead of multi-threaded resources. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic text processing, and low-intensity background utilities.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-1 is a general-purpose instance with 1 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPU and 4 GB RAM, offering strong single-core performance but limited memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-1 features 1 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPU and 4 GB RAM, delivering predictable single-core performance for light workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly, single-core instance with dedicated AMD EPYC resources and a balanced memory footprint for predictable, lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: Single dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 vCPU with x86_64 architecture
4.0 GB of system memory with no bundled local storage or GPUs
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Constrained multi-core performance and low memory bandwidth
Predictable dedicated CPU allocation for lightweight, single-threaded workloads
